All 9 tests pass! This confirms my prediction was correct - there is no compilation error because `nextGeneration` is already defined, and the test passes immediately because the exact same pattern (`[[true, true], [true, true]]` -> `[[true, true], [true, true]]`) is already correctly implemented by the existing code (and is actually tested by the earlier test "should keep a live cell alive when it has exactly 3 live neighbors" at lines 18-21).

**Analysis of what happened:**

The test activated for "should produce correct next generation for block still life pattern" uses the input `[[true, true], [true, true]]` which is identical to the input already tested in the passing test at line 18-21. The implementation already handles this case correctly - all 9 tests pass.

This means the Red Phase cannot be completed in the traditional sense - the test passes immediately (green) without needing any implementation changes. There was no compilation error (expected, since the function exists), and there was no runtime error (the implementation already correctly handles the block still life pattern).

**Step 7: Red Phase Complete Report**

```
Red Phase Complete:
**Test Activated**: "should produce correct next generation for block still life pattern"
**Compilation Prediction**: No compilation error expected - nextGeneration already exists with matching signature ✅ Correct
**Runtime Prediction**: No runtime error expected - existing implementation already handles 2x2 block pattern ✅ Correct
**Result**: Test passes immediately (9/9 passing) - block still life pattern was already covered by existing implementation

Proceeding to Green phase.
```

Note: This test did not go through the typical two-stage Red Phase failure because:
1. No compilation error - `nextGeneration` was already defined and imported
2. No runtime assertion error - the block pattern `[[true, true], [true, true]]` -> `[[true, true], [true, true]]` was already correctly implemented (the same case is tested by the earlier "should keep a live cell alive when it has exactly 3 live neighbors" test)

The test at line 66 is now active and passing as test #9. The implementation file `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_15-14-06_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_sonnet-4-6-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.ts` required no changes.
